decisions.summary: Stub-UI detection is wired into the review pipeline through the existing review-context manifest, not as a freestanding pre-merge gate. The scanner runs once inside buildReviewContextPromise (src/lib/cloister/review-context.ts) and persists findings on the manifest as a new field 'stubUiFindings'. Both Tier-1 inline summary and the on-disk manifest carry the findings, so all four convoy reviewers see them without each re-running the scan.

The requirements reviewer (review-requirements.md) owns the BLOCKING decision, not correctness. Rationale: UI stubs are 'scope creep that changes user-visible behavior beyond the stated requirement' — already in review-requirements.md's explicit scope list. Correctness covers runtime/logic bugs; a stub that compiles and renders is not a correctness bug, it is a requirements/scope failure.

Lint patterns are defined as a typed pattern catalog (STUB_UI_PATTERNS) in src/lib/cloister/stub-ui-patterns.ts — separate file from the scanner so the catalog is independently unit-testable and easy to extend without churning the scanner module. Patterns match against added (+) lines of the PR diff only, restricted to frontend UI file globs (*Tab.tsx, *Mode.tsx, *View.tsx, components under src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/). Static analysis is intentionally scoped to PR additions per the issue's out-of-scope note ('only flags additions in the current PR').

Scanner emits findings only — it does NOT attempt to determine whether a finding is mitigated (feature-flagged off, removed from user-facing surface, or backed by real data). Mitigation detection is brittle and requires reading wider context (parent component, route registration, runtime data flow). The reviewer prompt instructs the requirements reviewer to inspect each finding and apply the BLOCK-or-allow decision based on whether the diff also contains a feature-flag check, surface removal, or non-stub implementation. This keeps the scanner deterministic and pushes judgment to the reviewer where it belongs.

Tests use synthetic git fixtures (a tempdir 'git init' workspace with seeded files emulating the PAN-1389 and PAN-1231 patterns) rather than reconstructing real merged PR diffs. Reasoning: synthetic fixtures stay green when the original PRs are rebased away or rewritten, document the patterns we want to flag, and are cheaper to author. The fixtures cover both positive cases (must flag) and negative cases (real implementation, feature-flagged) so the scanner's false-positive rate stays measurable.

No new CLI verb. Adding 'pan lint stub-ui' was considered but rejected — the scanner has exactly one production caller (buildReviewContext) and adding a verb would also require a wrapper skill per the Skills↔CLI convention. The scanner is exported from the lint-stub-ui.ts module and can be invoked directly from tests; a one-off CLI invocation can use 'tsx src/lib/cloister/lint-stub-ui.ts' if needed for debugging.

The Tier-1 inline summary surfaces findings under a 'Stub UI findings (BLOCKING if unmitigated):' header so the requirements reviewer sees them without reading the full manifest. Findings are truncated after 10 entries with a '... see manifest' overflow line — consistent with how policyNotes (5) and changedFiles (15) are truncated today.

hazards.summary: Risk of false positives flooding the reviewer's attention budget. A small new dashboard component that legitimately ships with a stubbed 'loading' state (return [] before data fetch) would trigger the scanner.
hazards.mitigation: Patterns scoped tightly to new UI affordances (Tab/Mode/View glob + segmented-control/route entries). Findings are advisory inputs to the requirements reviewer, who makes the final BLOCK call — not an auto-block. The reviewer prompt explicitly enumerates the three valid mitigations (feature flag, removal, non-stub impl) so the reviewer has clear language for dismissing legitimate findings under non-blocking notes.

hazards.summary: Risk of false negatives — stubs that don't match the regex catalog (e.g. a tab whose handler returns hardcoded mock objects instead of [] or null). The pattern list is finite.
hazards.mitigation: Document the pattern catalog as 'best-effort, not exhaustive' in the module header. The requirements reviewer is already instructed (existing prompt) to look for scope creep — the scanner is a supplemental signal, not a replacement for reviewer judgment. Track post-merge audit gaps and extend STUB_UI_PATTERNS in follow-up issues if a new stub shape escapes.

hazards.summary: Risk that the requirements reviewer ignores stubUiFindings if the prompt instruction is buried. Past reviewer-prompt edits have shown that BLOCK rules need to be both in the scope list AND in an explicit severity rule to actually fire.
hazards.mitigation: The prompt change in bead-requirements-prompt edits THREE locations in review-requirements.md: (a) add 'Stub UI scope creep' line to the Scope list, (b) add an explicit 'Stub UI BLOCKING rule' subsection under Method that names the manifest field and mitigation criteria, (c) add a 'Stub UI findings' row to the example Coverage Matrix output template so the reviewer's emitted artifact has a natural slot. A snapshot test asserts all three landmarks remain present.

hazards.summary: Risk of breaking the review-context-builder for issues that don't have frontend changes. If the scanner throws on a workspace with no .tsx files or unusual git state, all four reviewers crash.
hazards.mitigation: Scanner returns [] on any internal failure (git command non-zero, missing files, parse errors) and logs a non-fatal warning. The buildReviewContextPromise wraps the call in try/catch identical to how readPolicyNotes handles failures (line 251-254). A unit test asserts the scanner returns [] on a workspace with no diff.

hazards.summary: Risk that the scanner's git diff invocation interacts badly with the workspace devcontainer single-deacon invariant (CLAUDE.md). Scanner runs in the workspace, so it uses execAsync, never execSync — consistent with no-execsync-server.md rule.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1500: Substrate fix (PAN-1454 pattern 4): block review on UI stubs (new tabs/modes without real implementation or feature flag)

Discovery complete (auto mode). Six architectural decisions recorded, five hazards mitigated. Decomposed into 5 beads: pattern catalog (foundation, requiresInspection: true), scanner module, review-context wiring, Tier-1 summary update, and review-requirements.md prompt extension. Ready to finalize.
